predicted_evidence.text:
I reviewed Emily Carter in clinic today following three months of intermittent brief events. They describe stereotyped episodes with a sudden sense of "switching off" and loss of awareness for 10–20 seconds, sometimes followed by a short period of confusion. There are no consistent focal auras reported. Injuries have not occurred. Family have witnessed two events and noted blank staring with unresponsiveness, without clear limb jerking.

Regarding frequency, the patient reports that episodes tend to come in small runs: typically around four to six short spells grouped together on days when they occur, most commonly later in the day towards the evening period. There may then be several days without events. Sleep disruption and higher anxiety levels appear to precede these clusters, and caffeine excess may contribute. Alcohol intake is modest and there is no recreational drug use.

Past medical history is notable for longstanding anxiety with intermittent low mood, currently improved on Sertraline 50 mg daily for the past six months, with no reported side effects. Screening today suggests stable mood; no suicidal ideation. They are open to psychological therapies and will discuss an IAPT referral with you.

On examination today, neurological assessment was unremarkable. Routine blood tests earlier this year were normal. We have arranged updated baseline bloods today. An MRI brain with epilepsy protocol has been requested, and a routine EEG has been scheduled; should events remain poorly captured, we would consider ambulatory monitoring.

Levetiracetam 500 mg twice daily was started by the emergency department six weeks ago after two clusters within a fortnight. Since initiation, the patient reports a reduction in event severity and number per cluster, with no new adverse effects beyond mild initial fatigue which has settled. We discussed adherence, common triggers, and the importance of sleep regularity. A seizure diary was provided to document timing (particularly evening tendency), precipitants, and recovery features to better guide further management.

We reviewed DVLA guidance and driving restrictions; the patient understands current requirements and will inform the DVLA as appropriate.

I will see Emily Carter again in approximately four months to review the imaging, EEG results, and diary data. Please feel free to contact me sooner if there is any clinical concern, a significant increase in clusters, or medication intolerance.
